Identity.Name: StingSteelGrades
Identity.Definition: Structural steel grades per BS EN 10025 / BS EN 10088. Drives structural design (yield strength, ultimate strength), section selection, embodied-carbon lookup, and Tekla connector handover. Includes both carbon-structural (S275..S690) and stainless (304 / 316) grades.

Governance.StandardsBasis: BS EN 10025-2:2019 (hot-rolled structural steel); BS EN 10088 (stainless); BS EN 1993 (Eurocode 3 design)

Value.Definition: S235 — yield strength 235 MPa. Light mild steel.
S275 — yield 275 MPa. General-purpose structural carbon steel.
S355 — yield 355 MPa. UK structural-steel default.
S550 — yield 550 MPa. Heavy / long-span applications.
S690 — yield 690 MPa. Very-high-strength steel; specialist welding procedures.
304 (1.4301) — austenitic stainless steel. Architectural + sanitary use.
316 (1.4401) — molybdenum-bearing austenitic; better chloride resistance. Marine / pharmaceutical / healthcare.
316L (1.4404) — low-carbon 316. Better weldability for thicker sections.
2205 (1.4462) — duplex stainless. High strength + chloride resistance; food / chemical / desalination.
Q235 — Chinese GB standard mild steel; approximate S235 equivalent.
Q355 — Chinese GB standard structural steel; approximate S355 equivalent.
A36 — US ASTM standard mild structural steel; yield 250 MPa.
A992 — US ASTM standard wide-flange shapes; yield 345 MPa.
